lesson.menu_desc: Meetings & Follow-Up — Reported Speech + Reporting Verbs — 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 4 — Reporting What Was Said — Meetings, Briefings, and Follow-Up Communication
lesson.title_tag: Professor View — Victor Malvezi Paschotto | Aula 4 — Reporting What Was Said

listenings.text: Good morning everyone. This is Karen Liu, Chief Operating Officer, with a brief update on our Q1 priorities. After reviewing the year-end results, the executive team has reached a consensus on three key directives. First, we are going to proceed with the LATAM expansion plan that Victor's team outlined last quarter. The board confirmed that the budget allocation would increase by twelve percent. Second, all regional leads must circulate their updated growth forecasts by January fifteenth. Leadership insisted that these forecasts include headcount projections and conversion rate targets. Third, we are restructuring the digital platform team. The CTO mentioned that he had already begun the hiring process, and he suggested that each region commit at least two senior engineers to the project. I want to clarify one important point: the restructuring does not mean budget cuts. The CFO acknowledged that some teams were concerned about this, but she confirmed that all current budgets would remain intact. Finally, I want to debrief on the client escalation from last week. The client warned us that if we did not improve delivery timelines, they might consider other providers. We need to bring about change in our operations workflow immediately. That is all for now. Please confirm your attendance for next Thursday's follow-up. Thank you.

extra_audio.text: First, state who the meeting was with and when it took place. Second, report the main decisions that were made. Third, mention any concerns or objections that were raised. Fourth, outline the action items and deadlines. Fifth, confirm the next steps and who is responsible for each.
